title: GitHub #72: Export hangs/freezes on issue ANON-997 (Jira Cloud)
description:
GitHub issue: https://github.com/mikebowler/jirametrics/issues/72
Reporter: SimoneCusimano (Jira Cloud, Windows, Ruby 3.4). Email contact also possible via mbowler@gargoylesoftware.com.

## Symptom (current state)
Export FREEZES (no exception, no progress) on the same issue every run: ANON-997. Confirmed it freezes even with a clean/emptied target folder. Problem started in v2.28 (v2.27 works fine for them). v2.28 changelog: "We now download issues that were linked from the primary issues so that we can provide better context."

## History of the bug (it has shifted over time)
1. Originally: crash with "status references id that can't be found" — fixed by switching to warning + guessing category (2.29.3pre3).
2. Then: `curl (56) Recv failure: Connection was reset` during bulk_fetch_issues — added retry logic (2.29.3pre4).
3. Then: `id cannot be nil` RuntimeError in Status#initialize via fabricate_status_for (2.30) — addressed in 2.30.1pre1.
4. Then: silent HANG. No exception. This is the current problem.

## Fixes already shipped (none resolved the hang)
- 2.30.1pre6: `break if worklogs.empty?` guard in attach_worklogs_to_issues (worklog pagination infinite loop hypothesis). RULED OUT — user's log had zero worklog pagination lines.
- 2.30.1pre7 (commit bcd7f4d): Fixed O(n^2) in the attach phase. The three attach_* methods (attach_subtasks/attach_parent/attach_linked_issues) in project_config.rb each used all_issues.find {...} (O(n)) called per-issue. With v2.28 linked-issue downloading inflating issue count to tens of thousands, this was O(n^2). Now builds key->issue hash once for O(1) lookup. User confirmed STILL FREEZES after this fix, even with clean target folder.

## Current diagnostic step (commit 53fe1ed, will ship as pre8)
Added [diag] log checkpoints to pinpoint WHERE it hangs:
- downloader_for_cloud.rb download_issues loop: logs "total known / stale / link-scanned" counts each iteration of related-issues phase.
- project_config.rb issues(): logs before/after loading from disk, before/after attach phase ("Starting attach phase" / "Attach phase complete"), after primary-issue filtering ("Retained N primary issues").
- project_config.rb discard_changes_before(): logs "processing N issues".
NEXT: ask user to install pre8, reproduce, send last ~20 lines of jirametrics.log. The last [diag] line before freeze identifies the phase.

## Key evidence
- ANON-997 (id 155971) is a Task in project ANON, board 57/183. It has 16+ issuelinks (Relates, Tests, Affect) spanning OTHER projects: ANON, ANON2, ANON3. Full JSON pasted in the GitHub issue comments.
- Cloners-type links are excluded by collect_related_issue_keys (downloader_for_cloud.rb:321), but Relates/Tests/Affect are all followed transitively.
- collect_related_issue_keys follows parent + subtasks + ALL non-Cloner issuelinks. This can transitively pull in the entire instance across projects/boards.
- User also reported warnings about "unexpected issue keys" from projects the board shouldn't pull from — consistent with transitive cross-project link following.
- Earlier private log (report_anonimizzato.txt, NOT committed) had 14031 lines: 1 "Loading historical statuses" + 14030 "status change without to id" warnings across 7270+ distinct issues, 20+ project prefixes. Zero worklog lines.

## Leading hypotheses still open
1. Download-phase related-issues loop never converges OR is just extremely slow due to transitive cross-project/cross-board link explosion (could be tens of thousands of issues, each a curl call). pre8 diag will show if total-known keeps growing.
2. Some remaining O(n^2) or slow operation in export AFTER attach phase (e.g. discard_changes_before, chart generation) now that attach is O(1).
3. A genuine infinite loop somewhere not yet identified.

## Files of interest
- lib/jirametrics/downloader_for_cloud.rb: download_issues (loop ~231), collect_related_issue_keys (~312), attach_worklogs_to_issues (~133), bulk_fetch_issues (~88)
- lib/jirametrics/project_config.rb: issues (~465), attach_* (~505-524), discard_changes_before (~612)
- lib/jirametrics/issue_collection.rb: extends Array, find is O(n)
- lib/jirametrics/status_collection.rb: find_by_id O(n), fabricate_status_for

## Constraints
- Cannot reproduce locally — depends on user's Jira instance. Diagnostics-driven.
- Data Centre code is deprecated; do not modify. This is Cloud (DownloaderForCloud).
notes: Waiting on user (SimoneCusimano) to install 2.30.1pre8 and send last ~20 lines of jirametrics.log showing the last [diag] line before the freeze. Expected response in 1-2 days from 2026-06-20. The related transient-HTTP-error issue is jirametrics-zfy.

title: Improve error messages for transient HTTP errors
description: When Jira returns transient errors like 503 (Service Unavailable), the current output is a raw curl exit code and stack trace. This is confusing even for the developer who wrote the code, and will be worse for casual users. We should catch these HTTP error codes and display a human-friendly message explaining that the error is temporary and the user should try again later. Note that a 503 can also mean the Jira account has been deactivated due to inactivity — the error message should mention this as a possibility too.
